evals.prompt: I want to propose migrating our monolithic Django app to microservices. Our CTO is skeptical and wants to see a business case. We have 15 engineers, the app serves 50K users, and deployments take 2 hours with frequent rollbacks. Help me write the proposal.
evals.expected_output: Should produce a concise proposal with clear problem statement (deployment pain, not just 'microservices are trendy'), alternatives including 'do nothing' and 'modular monolith', honest risk assessment, and measurable success criteria.
assertions: Frames the problem as deployment pain and team scalability, not 'microservices are better'
Includes 'do nothing' and 'modular monolith' as alternatives
Provides rough effort estimate in person-weeks with a range
Risk assessment includes distributed system complexity and team skill gaps
Success criteria are measurable (deploy time, rollback rate, team velocity)
Keeps the proposal to 1-2 pages equivalent
Does NOT assume microservices are the right answer — presents it objectively

evals.prompt: Write a project proposal for adding a real-time chat feature to our SaaS product. Budget approval needed from the VP of Engineering.
evals.expected_output: Should ask clarifying questions about the business case before writing, then produce a structured proposal.
assertions: Asks about the business justification (why chat? user demand data?)
Asks about build vs buy (Sendbird, Stream, custom WebSocket)
Includes build vs buy as alternatives
Estimates account for ongoing operational cost (not just build cost)
Risk section addresses real-time infrastructure complexity
